name: Critical File Deleted
notes:
This rule trigggers when a critical file or file in a critical directory is deleted. The swap files are excluded by default and any other file extensions that you do not want to monitor can be excluded as well.

Note: Edit the "BB:CategoryDefinition: Critical Files and Directories for Endpoint" Building Block with files or directories to monitor. These locations should be related to boot, backup, logging, or credentials, which have a higher severity when exploited.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Multiple File Deletions on Endpoint System
BB:CategoryDefinition: Critical Files and Directories for Endpoint
conditions_text: when an event matches all of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System, BB:CategoryDefinition: Critical Files and Directories for Endpoint, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Multiple File Deletions on Endpoint System
TD_list: APPLY Critical File Deleted on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when an event matches all of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System, BB:CategoryDefinition: Critical Files and Directories for Endpoint, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Multiple File Deletions on Endpoint System
RED_list: Event description: Detects when a critical file or file in a critical directory is deleted.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
